sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 37, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves avatar +3 cols right, charcoal also +3 cols right (VERIFIED steps 1-4)', 'LEFT': 'Moves avatar -3 cols left, charcoal also -3 cols left (VERIFIED steps 5-6)', 'DOWN': 'Moves avatar +3 rows down, charcoal also +3 rows down (VERIFIED steps 7-10)', 'UP': 'Moves avatar -3 rows up, charcoal also -3 rows up (HYPOTHESIS)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'black_with_dots': 'Player avatar - black 6x6 shape with 5 white dot features, moves with arrow keys', 'charcoal_shape': 'Charcoal object that moves same direction as avatar by 3 pixels, initially at offset', 'yellow_shape': 'Target object at (45,51), 45 pixels, L-shaped, 6x6', 'sky_blue_strip': 'Vertical divider at cols 30-32, changes shape as objects cross it'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_row': 'Black strip at row 63, border', 'right_col': 'Yellow strip at col 63, border'}
Current level:
- World model: Avatar overshot target and merged with bottom black strip (node 7, 103 pixels, rows 54-63, cols 0-62). Target yellow at (45,51). Avatar likely stuck at bottom edge.
- Goal model: Move avatar to overlap with yellow target at (45,51)
- Recent findings: ['Avatar moved too far down and merged with bottom border', 'Need to go UP to reach target row 45']
- Open questions: ['What completes the level - overlap with target?', 'Can avatar escape bottom border merge?', 'Does SPACE or MOUSE do something special?']
- Plan: Try UP moves to get avatar back to target row. If UP doesn't work, try SPACE or MOUSE.
- Strategy log: ['Overshot target by going DOWN 7 + RIGHT 6 from (33,42) to (54,60), then corrected with UP 3 + LEFT 3']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 36 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 1.1x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
SPACE didn't complete the level. Let me check what changed after SPACE. The board changed but level is not complete. Let me look at the current segmentation to understand what happened and figure out what the goal actually is.
Looking at the image, the avatar is still at the same position, adjacent to the yellow target. The target seems to have some yellow dots inside the avatar now. Maybe the goal is to fully overlap the avatar with the target, not just be adjacent.
Current avatar: (45,45), target: (45,54)
The target is 9 columns to the right. I need to move RIGHT by 3 to get the avatar to (45,48), then another RIGHT to (45,51), then another RIGHT to (45,54) to fully overlap.
Wait, let me reconsider. The avatar is 6x6, so it occupies cols 45-50. The target is at col 54, so it starts at col 54. There's a gap of 3 columns between them (cols 51-53). I need to move RIGHT once to close that gap.
Actually, looking more carefully at the adjacency list from before, the avatar (node 6) was adjacent to the yellow target (node 7). So they're touching. But the target is at (45,54) and avatar at (45,45). If avatar is 6x6, it occupies cols 45-50. Target at col 54 means there's a gap of cols 51-53. But they're adjacent... maybe the target extends leftward?
Let me just try moving RIGHT to see if the avatar overlaps more with the target.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("Current objects:")
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] > 10 and n['color'] in ['B', 'c', 'Y']:
        b = n['boundary']
        r, c = min([p[0] for p in b]), min([p[1] for p in b])
        print(f"  {n['color']}: top=({r},{c}), pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Avatar at (45,45), target at (45,54)
# Need to move RIGHT to overlap target
# Try RIGHT to close the gap
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves avatar +3 cols right (VERIFIED)", "LEFT": "Moves avatar -3 cols left (VERIFIED)", "DOWN": "Moves avatar +3 rows down (VERIFIED)", "UP": "Moves avatar -3 rows up (VERIFIED)", "SPACE": "Did not complete level (step 36)"}, "object_taxonomy": {"black_with_dots": "Player avatar", "charcoal_shape": "Moves with avatar", "yellow_shape": "Target at (45,54), 18 pixels"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_row": "Black strip", "right_col": "Yellow strip"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": ""}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Avatar at (45,45), target at (45,54), adjacent. SPACE didn't complete level.", "goal_model": "Fully overlap avatar with target, or find another completion condition", "recent_findings": ["SPACE did not complete level"], "open_questions": ["What completes the level?"], "current_plan": "Move RIGHT to overlap target more", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["SPACE at step 36"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
